Hey everyone, this is Russell Brunson. Welcome back to the 10X Secrets Masterclass. Now this session is going to be going through exactly how the slide deck works, how the framework for the presentation works, and a whole bunch of other amazing things. So I'm not going to lie, I was a little bit nervous to give you guys this because in the past I've given my slides to a couple people and if you knew how much time and energy and effort goes into creating these things you'd understand why I don't normally give these out to anybody but we thought let's make everyone's jobs a lot easier. Now the one thing I do request is when you use these slides, make sure you use your own images. I get people all the time who come in here and grab them, they'll steal the images that I've used for different things and you have to understand like these, first off a lot of these images are mine, I've designed them or I've paid for these images so it's actually legal to do that and especially people who say I bought images from, if I bought images from Shutterstock or iStock photo, things like that, if you're grabbing them and using them they will come after you and they are their mean companies. I'm a nice company, they can be mean. So just know that, make sure you're using your own images and things for your own slides and make sure that you're using this as a template but customize it for your own, okay? That's a big key hit. So a couple of things I want to kind of go through before I dive into the actual slides. Number one, this presentation is not going to be an in-depth training of exactly how to do all these things, that's all in the 10x Secrets training. So make sure you go back and watch all that training, go understand it, go master it because in there I'm going to go into the really nitty gritties behind the strategy and the techniques and how you give the presentation. This is more so just so you guys see how this framework fits into the slide deck as you start going and editing your slides, okay? The next thing, this is right here, this is the framework of how the perfect webinar works and if you really want to go deeper into it, get the expert secrets book. The whole book is about this framework and we go really deep into every single piece of it. So if you want to go into the levels and case studies and just if you want to just understand and master it at an even deeper level, then make sure you read that book. And then down here, this is also from the expert secrets book. These are the Epiphany Bridge script. This is how we tell our stories throughout the webinars, okay? And so understanding, I'm going to kind of, I'll kind of reference some of these things during this training today, but if you want to go deep into it, again, it's going into 10x Secrets training, going into the expert secrets book, that's where you're going to get to really master and understand how to do these things. Also inside of the 10x Secrets training, there's a whole section called Story Selling Secrets, which is where I go through the Epiphany Bridge script and I talk about story and how to like elicit emotion when you're telling your stories and how to tell your backstory and how that leads to the internal and the external desires and I kind of go deep into this whole process, okay? And so make sure you're using all those resources. This video is not to go deep into the training, it's just to help you to understand how to plug these things into the presentation and how they all kind of fit together. That sound good? All right, so I'm going to spend a minute real quick on the framework. So this is the framework of the perfect webinar, okay? And it's broken down into a couple sections. Number one is your intro where you're building rapport with somebody and then from there, you're normally telling your origin story, okay? It's the very first shot of you kind of telling your story about why you believe that this thing you've created is the greatest thing on planet Earth and it's going to give somebody the result they're looking for and it's going to change their life, okay? So that's typically the first 15 minutes is your intro, you're building rapport and you're telling your origin story, okay? And from there, we transition to the content section of the training. Now if you see how I structure my content, there's always like, here's the one thing I'm trying to convince you of and then here's the three secrets to that, okay? So the one thing for me is like, I want to convince you that funnels are going to be the thing that will change your business. It's going to change your life. It gets the key, right? For you, the one thing is going to be different. It could be, and again, if you want to really understand this at a deep level, I highly recommend reading the expert secrets book. Your one thing may be, you know, your way of losing weight is the most important thing or whatever it is, right? So I have my one thing and then my three secrets, okay? And just kind of as a reminder and a recap, the three secrets are all about the three different faults, like the core faults beliefs that keeps somebody from buying from you. Does that make sense? So the first faults belief that they have that keeps them from buying is their faults beliefs about the vehicle that you're offering them. So the vehicle is the opportunity, the product, the service, the thing you're trying to get them into. It's faults beliefs about that, okay? So for me, if I'm trying to, like, typically people I'm speaking to, they're trying to grow a company. And so I'm coming to them and the vehicle I'm offering them is saying, hey, the vehicle I have is this thing called a funnel and it'll grow your company faster than anything else you could possibly do, okay? So that's the vehicle. So I have to come and say, and then they may look at it and say, well, that's cool, Russell, but I'm spending time over here on hiring a team and doing that. And they have all these other options they're trying to do. And I have to convince them, no, no, no, like this vehicle, the funnel, is the most important thing you could possibly be doing through time right now, okay? This is the key for you getting the result you're looking for. So that's the first thing I got to convince them of the vehicle, okay? Then after I convince them of the vehicle, then naturally what happens after that, cool, that makes sense. I think the vehicle is right. And the second fault split that always pops up is like, well, the funnel seemed awesome, but like, I don't know if I could do that, right? Like I'm not technical, I don't know how to design things, blah, blah, blah. And so the second fault split I have to smash is their internal fault splits about themselves being able to execute on the vehicle that I'm offering them, okay? So if you're helping someone, like, I'm helping you lose weight, I'm helping you, you know, whatever it is you're selling, right? The thing about what are the fault splits they have about themselves personally, you have to break second, right? That's the second secret. And the third secret, like what normally will happen is they'll say, okay, funnels are a great thing in the world. I think I could actually use click funnels, it seems awesome, but I don't know how to drive traffic. I don't know, you know, they always have some external thing that's keeping them out, like they're blaming something else, like, well, I can't, I'm not going to success because of that, right? See, I feel, what is that that? And your third thing you're teaching in your presentation, and the third set of stories you're telling are breaking the false belief around that, the external thing is keeping them from success. So for my business, the external thing that is keeping them from success is traffic, okay? For yours, it's going to be something different. So you have to figure out what's the external thing that's keeping them from success, and then you're telling stories and breaking false beliefs around that, okay? So these each take about 15 minutes. So if you look at the timeline of this presentation, this, the intro and me telling my origin story is the first 15 minutes, okay? Secret one's about 15 minutes, secret two 15 minutes, secret three's 15 minutes. I'm about an hour into this, okay? Then I transition to my stack of my clothes. I'll spend 30 minutes on my stack of my clothes. So I'm at a live event that I'm spending 30 minutes doing that. So I'm at, you know, I'm at an hour up here and 30 minutes down here. So that makes 90. If I'm, if I'm, if I'm doing a webinar, I'll spend 30 minutes here and I'll do another 30 minutes to Q&A, which shows me out at about two hours. Now, if I'm doing like a Facebook live hack like Kailan did, or if I'm doing a shorter version for e-commerce like, like Jamie Cross did, I'm just compressing these things down. Right? I'm still using the framework. The framework does not shift. It does not transition. It's perfect. Like don't, that's what we call the perfect webinars. Perfect. Don't mess with it. But it might shrink, okay? I might go, if I, if Kailan does her perfect webinar hack, she does it in 30 minutes through Facebook live, right? So everything's still here. She's just telling shorter stories. She's compressing things, but the framework stays intact. Same thing with Jamie Cross did it for her soap, okay? The framework is the same. It just shrinks. The story shrinks, the time shrinks, but the framework is intact. Okay? So that's a big key to understand. Now, the other thing, and hopefully you've gone through the rest of the training because it'll, it'll make more sense if you have, but the majority of what I'm doing through my presentation is I'm telling stories. Telling stories to teach them something and to break a false belief. Okay? And so then the question comes is like, well, what's, how do I tell stories? What's the, what's the story arc? So if you've read the expert secrets book, I talk about this concept called the epiphany bridge. And the epiphany bridge is how we tell stories. And it's not something I made up. This is like how all stories from the beginning of time till now work, right? To get all the highest growth, the most successful movies of all time, they all follow the same story arc. Okay? And so this is what this whole thing is here right here. And so I'm going to be showing this throughout the presentation. I'm doing this over and over and over and over again. And sometimes I'm going to tell a story like Hollywood might tell a story. It takes two to three hours in a movie to get through this whole story arc, right? If you're like in a TV show, like the show 24 or, you know, whatever your show is like 24 does the story arc over 24 episodes of 24 hours. Everyone takes them tell the story arc. Okay? Katelyn does it in Facebook live in 30 minutes. Jamie does it in five minutes, right? So sometimes stories will be, you can compress and you can shrink them, but the framework doesn't shift. Okay? That's easy to understand is like the framework can compress and shrink based on how much details, how much do you want to tell the story, but the framework doesn't change. Don't mess with the framework. It's perfect. That's what we call the perfect web at art. Okay? So this is the framework of stories. If you're like, I don't know how to tell a story, Russell. These questions here will walk you through this process, right? And so I'm just going to leave these on the screens for going through so you can kind of see how these things kind of plug in. Okay? Now again, with these slides, I'm going to be walking you through, this is just a framework. You can add slides, you can delete slides, you can change things, you can tweak things. This is just to kind of give you a framework of how I weave all my stories in through this process. Okay? How I transition this framework into PowerPoint slides. Okay? So the very first I begin with is my title slide. So here's two examples of title slides I have. Two of my title slides are just a headline and a picture, right? Something to kind of grab their attention. But they're very, very simple. By the way, you'll notice most of my slides are simple. Don't over, don't spend money on over designing. I usually have a footer with like a logo and then she's usually a picture and a headline. That's it. If it's too designed and doing too many things, people will be focusing on your slides, not you as a presenter. And so you don't want to make it too fancy. I just have things to really, like really the goal is to remind me, like what's next in the slide? What's the next thing I need to say? It helps keep me on storyline because you don't want to come off script. Okay? Perfect webinar is perfect, but if you're in the middle of secret number one or you go off on this tangent for 25 minutes, you'll break the framework. So stay in the framework and the slides are there to guide you through the framework. Okay? As you get better at this though, you start, you'll stop needing the slides because you'll know this framework in your head. If you watch when Kalen did her perfect webinar hack on Facebook, she didn't have slides. She's had a piece of paper for secret number one, piece of paper for secret number two, piece of paper for secret number three, and the stack was up on her whiteboard, right? So she held a piece of paper and then she's told the story she knew she needed to tell. Held this to number two, told the stories, held number three, told the stories, and then did the stack and the close. Okay? So slides are just really there to guide me to keep me on script. Okay? The worst thing you can do as a presenter is get off of script. Just stay on script. Do not get off script. Okay? And that's what they're there for. And then my goal is that I don't want them to, it's so confusing though the audience gets sucked into them. I want them just basically there to give me reminders where I'm going and just keep it, keep them engaged, right? So that's why you'll notice the design's minimal. Okay? So I have a headline, whatever my headline or my presentation is there, then I start transitioning to the intro report. Okay? So in here I'm teaching, I'm introing, and I'm just, for me I'm telling jokes, right? So I tell a joke of like, I'm going to teach you how something's amazing, but don't use these powers for evil, right? I'm just trying to like, just to get them to like me. So I'm telling the story. In here I have an earnings disclaimer, but if you look at this earnings disclaimer, you may notice something. This is just a mini epiphany bridge with the goal of trying to build rapport. Okay? So I come out saying, hey guys, I'm going to show you how I make, you know, $300,000 a day. People are going to be like, he's an arrogant, annoying person who I hate, right? You don't want to do that. Instead I tell a story that builds rapport and gets them to know me. So I'm like, hey guys, you know, in the presentation I talked about up here, how we're making $17,000 a day, which is pretty awesome, right? But like, I don't know about you, but first I want to make sure, you know, like you're not going to make $17,000 a day, right? Like I just want to make sure we're all clear with that. This is my earnings disclaimer. Like that's not the guarantee of this presentation. I'm just trying to show you like a funnel we have that actually does that. In fact, for me, when I got started online, boom, notice I'm slipping immediately into backstory. When I got started online, I didn't want to make $17,000 a day. I just wanted to cover some bills. Like I had house payments, I had car payments, and blah, blah, blah, blah. I start telling my backstory and I moved this thing. I tell an entire Pifny Bridge script in like two minutes, right? I wanted to sing. I had a thing. I found it out. This is the goal is to make $2,739 a day. And if you times out by a year, it's a million bucks. If I were to make $2,739 a day, that'd be a million dollars, which is awesome. And so that was my goal. And for you guys, I said a goal like that, like how much money can you make per day? And then every day, give yourself a raise. Now what's cool is that now fast forward 10 years, I've got 27 active sales funnels. And some of them are doing horrible. Like some of them only make 20 bucks a day, but I've got a few. Like my biggest one I'm going to show you guys today, make $17,947 per day. Is that amazing? All right, cool. Okay, do you notice how like that story endeared me to you as opposed to made me annoying? If I said I got a funnel spending 17 grand a day, you're like, oh, screw you. I don't like you Russell. I'm like, I was broke. I want to be millionaire. And like, this is my goal. And then I'm also not doing an income plan, not showing them how much money they're going to make. But I just, I paint this vision of just like, hey, that's what you make that much a day. You make it. You're making a million bucks. You know, I've got 27 funnels and make, you know, all make somewhere in there. And so in their mind, they're like, oh my gosh, Russell's, Russell's making a lot. Right. So it's building rapport. It's also like establishing my credibility in a cool way that doesn't make me feel like a jerk. Okay. I tell another story because I'm trying to like, do this intro. I'm trying to grab them and keep their attention. So I tell a story about there's a student named Trey who's on this, the same webinar a year ago and let me show you his results. Right. So I'm kind of just like, this is why it's important for you to be on this webinar. Do not leave if you heard in the past and you're back and you didn't take action last time. Like stay on at this time because look what happened to Trey. He was just like you. And now because of he listened to this, he's having so much success. Okay. All right. Now I come over here and this is where I start introducing the new opportunity. Right. And, and so what I like doing here is I try to be inclusive. I want to try to include everybody's possible. Right. So who is this for? And what's nice about this, you can actually use this, this slide to like include people but also to exclude people. Right. So if I'm like, Hey, because otherwise people will get on your webinar and the one of the big thoughts is like, is this for me and they're trying to fill it out? Like, should I stay on this? Should I not? It's going to be an hour. My time is worth it. So you have to like say, look, if you, how many guys who are listening to have a business? Well, cool. If you have a business, this is for you because I'm going to show you how to use funnels to 10 X any business you got. Now how many guys are in startup phase and don't have a business yet? Cool. And better spot means you haven't screwed up in my bad habit. I'm going to show you guys how to use this funnel hacking process to launch your company right the very first time. So if you don't have a business, you have a business, this is for you. Okay. So I use that to make sure that everyone's like, Oh, cool. I'm in the right spot. Okay. Now at the same time, if there's like a group of people you don't want to serve like this, you're like, I don't want people who do blah, blah, blah to be, I don't want them to buy my stuff. You can exclude them here. I'm like, Hey, if you're making less than 50 grand a month right now, this is not for you. Right. If you're already in shape, this is not for you. And you can exclude people here as well, which helps. So I include the people I want to exclude the people I don't want. Okay. Now I come back. Here's the goal for the presentation. Okay. This is the big thing. I'm the one thing I'm trying to convince you of. And so I talked about a funnel a little bit. So for me, the thing I got to convince them is that the only way to like 10x your, your company is through sales funnels. So my entire presentation, my goal is to get you guys to understand this. And then I'm going to show you how the only way to do that is actually through click funnels. Okay. Does that sound good? All right. So I did a whole price marinate training. If you can, if you convinced me that funnels will 10x my business the next 12 months. Okay. So I plugged that in right here. And now we start transitioning to qualifying yourself. You know, a lot of people do in the sections, they talk about how cool they are and that's super annoying and nobody will like you afterwards. Okay. So what I do is I want to establish really quick that I am an authority, but I just touch upon it and I come back off it. Right. So I'm like, Hey, I'm Russell Brunson. I had a chance to build funnels for guys like Tony Robbins, the Spokanev's event. Here's me, my family and Fiji on top of this house, which was insane. And you look at how big his head is compared to mine. And he's like a giant. Awesome. Right. And then here's me with Richard Branson. He puts in my name on the side of the plane. It was awesome. What most of you guys are going to want to do is you want to be like, keep talking about how great you are. Don't you touch it so they're aware of how great you are, but then you come back to them as quick as possible. And this is where I tell my origin story. So the origin story is probably of all the stories, the one I'm going to go the deepest into the epiphany bridge script. Okay. I'm going to spend the most time here. I usually, and this is probably 10 minutes for me to tell this story alone. Right. But what's happening is that they just saw how awesome I am. They saw, oh my gosh, Richard Branson put Russell's book on the side of his plane. That must be, he must be the man. Right. But now it's like, if I'm not careful, it's going to distance themselves from me. So instantly you come back to where they are and I come into my epiphany bridge story, to my backstory. Because my back stories and picking up the exact same spot they're probably at right now. Okay. And here I start telling my backstory. So if you look at this, if you're doing your back, if you're doing your epiphany bridge story, this is the questions you got to answer. What's your backstory? It gives us a vested interest in your journey. So here I'm like, all right. So let me tell you guys how I got started though. Like my very first website was this thing called how to make a potato gun. How many of you guys have ever made a potato gun before? Right. And they're thinking, a potato gun? Like, are you kidding me? And it tells backstory. Okay then. But what was I trying to accomplish? What were my external desires? Like I want to make money because like blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. But what were my internal desires? Well, my internal desires was like, my wife was working at the time. I was going to school. I was providing nothing to the bottom line. I felt guilty and I wanted to like to be the man of the house. Like, you know, my wife and like I wanted, I wanted to be that person and I wasn't. So I had to figure out some other way to make money because I was wrestling at the time. I couldn't quit wrestling. And so I try to figure this out. So I tell my, my desires, right? And then I launched my first potato gun website and it was awesome. I put it out there and first it works, which was like this huge thing. And then Google came and slapped us and stopped working and blah, blah, blah. So I hit this wall, right? And so I had to figure something out and I was going to these things and then I met this friend, he told me a story and also I had this epiphany. It was this thing called upsell. I had an upsell of my product and all of a sudden boom, it started working. It was amazing. Okay. So I'm telling you the epiphany I had. And then after I figured that out, I was like, okay, what's my plan? Well, potato guns aren't a huge market. Maybe I can make, you know, 10 grand a year off this, but where else can I use? Like now I know funnels are the secret. What else can I do this with? And I was like, I did it here, here, here. I show all these different places I did build funnels, right? So here's my plan. And it was awesome. I was making like some made a ton of money, some made a little bit of money, but I became obsessed next 10 years building funnels like crazy. Like I realized it was the only thing on earth that actually made money consistently. So I was just doing it like crazy. But the problem, so here's my conflict was like, it got really expensive. I ended up having an office with over 100 people in there all helping me build funnels. And it was hard. It was painful. And until we developed a thing called click funnels and what's cool about that. And they talk about the two transformations, right? So if you haven't gone through the story selling stuff yet, go back in there. But talk about it up here, we set up our internal external desires. Really your achievement is you are fulfilling your external desires. So it's like now we're able to make funnels. It used to take us three months to build a funnel. Now we can do it in about a week. We can do it in an hour, we can do it in a day, whatever. And we're making way more money and we were able to cut our staff down to about 95%. Like we would have one person for every 10 we had in the past. Boom achievement. Then it's like the transformational journey addresses the internal. So not only was that awesome, the cooler thing is the fact that because I'm not spending all the time now running a team of 100 people building funnels. I can spend more time with my wife and my kids and that's the real power behind what we're talking about. Boom, so I tell the whole Pifty Bridge story. So now it happened a couple of things. So for a lot of you guys, just the intro alone is enough to sell people. But it's kind of the key. Now, yeah, the origin story is so powerful. I'm putting those things and dying in deep to really build rapport with them. Okay. Then I show case study of like really quick, like, look guys, this is the hard way. This is what funnels used to do in the past. This is the, this is the way that we did it. So I'm 15 minutes in on slide 20. Well, not slide 29 because these are my little buffers, but you see what I'm doing so far. It's, it's to do the first pass and convincing them that the vehicle that I'm going to be putting them in is the greatest thing on planet earth. Okay. All right. Secrets about the vehicle, about their internal ability to execute on the vehicle and the external things that think will keep them back from success. Like I'm just looking for something sexy for these headlines. So people are like so intrigued. They have to like watch the presentation. They can't cancel if they want to, right? So secret ones called funnel hacking, how to ethically store over a million dollars with the funnel actually being paid for under a hundred bucks. Secret number two, funnel cloning, how to then clone the proven funnel. Secret number three, right? So I'm just, I'm telling these cool things where they're just like, oh my gosh, this looks so cool. I need it. So I touch bottom three things, what we're going to talk about. And then I jump in and start talking about it. Boom. Secret number one. And then I tell my pitfing bridge story about that. Okay. Now, I'm not going to spend time going through my slides because hopefully you see my presentation or seen variation of it. All I'm doing is like, I'm coming back here to the vehicle, right? Now again, I told my origin story about why I believe about the vehicle. And then I'm going to tell this another story I can tell to get them to believe that for me, funnels are the grace in the world. I'm coming back and telling a story. What's the story I can plug into this framework? So for me, the story was, was this whole funnel hacking principle, right? So I went back and I went from this guy Tony Robbins. I'm going to my backstory and like you model people and this is what pioneers did. And I found someone to funnel hack and then I did this and I did that. I tell this whole story, this journey I went upon and like, I realized like, I don't have to like build a funnel. I just find someone else's funnel. I model it and boom, I'm successful. Okay. So I tell that whole story. And so at first people like, Oh my gosh, like they, this is the teaching moment, right? I'm teaching them this process. Like you have to be smart. You just go find someone else, you buy their product, you see behind the scenes. Now you have a blueprint of what you actually create. This is your business blueprint. Okay. So I'm teaching him something when I'm wrapping it in a story. So it's exciting, entertaining. They're going to remember it. And after it's done, then I'm like, but look, it's not just me. Let me show other people. Like here's how drew it with organifying. Here's how Jamel did it. So I'm showing them more proof of other people besides me doing the same thing. Also like, Oh my gosh, like it's not just Russell. And then the next thought in people's heads is like, well, you know, maybe it's, they worked for Russell and Drew, but not for, not for me. It's probably going to be at least a year. Um, for Kailin was a year, right? Like, um, and then after you've mastered it and you've told these stories so many times, you know how to tell them, you know, the concerns, you know, the false beliefs you've plugged them in your slides, they've gotten better and better and better and better.
